pattern: The four allowed jumps form a system where always taking the biggest jump that still fits never costs more than any other order, because each smaller jump size divides evenly into the next one up.
hints: Convert both times to a single number of minutes since midnight and work with just the difference between them.
Because 60 is a multiple of 15, 15 is a multiple of 5, and 5 is a multiple of 1, using as many of the largest jump as possible before moving to the next smaller size never wastes a jump.
Take the difference divided by 60 (rounded down) as the number of hour-jumps, then repeat the same 'take as many as fit' step on the remainder with 15, then 5, then 1, summing the counts.

edge_inputs.description: current already equals correct, zero operations needed
the maximum possible gap across a full day
a gap requiring a mix of every operation size
a gap of exactly one minute
a gap that is a clean multiple of sixty minutes
a gap that is a clean multiple of fifteen minutes

stress.description: many random valid time pairs across the full day
